title: podocarpus maki
intro: Podocarpus maki, also known as Maki or Japanese Podocarpus, is a type of coniferous evergreen tree native to Japan and Taiwan. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving coughs and sore throats, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Podocarpus maki is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its resin is sometimes used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat respiratory issues.
